verbatim_note: All titles/urls are from AGENT 4's OWN real WebSearch results; independent of AGENT 3; no fabrication.

candidates.notes: MISMATCH: my fresh searches surfaced a direct collision. 'ACCessory: password inference using accelerometers on smartphones' (Owusu et al., HotMobile '12, https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/2162081.2162095) uses the same-device accelerometer (a permissionless sensor) to infer text typed on the phone's own soft keyboard via machine learning. TapLogger (https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/2185448.2185465) does the same for touchscreen taps. These match the candidate's core idea, so AGENT 3's 'no collision' is wrong.

recheck_searches.query: inferring smartphone touchscreen input from on-board accelerometer vibrations deep learning

candidates.notes: CONFIRMS AGENT 3: my two fresh searches found no paper casting crop-row localization as differentiable-renderer analysis-by-synthesis with a parametric periodic row model fit by gradient descent for label-free sub-pixel accuracy. Existing work is Hough transform, supervised segmentation, or Fourier/frequency analysis. No paper-like result with a matching title appeared.

recheck_searches.query: analysis-by-synthesis differentiable renderer crop row detection self-supervised aerial imagery
parametric periodic row model gradient descent sub-pixel localization vineyard UAV without labels

candidates.notes: CONFIRMS AGENT 3: my two fresh searches found no paper that switches quantization bit-width in response to real-time SoC/skin temperature for on-device LLM inference. Thermal-aware DVFS and adaptive/mixed-precision quantization exist but are treated separately (DVFS adjusts clock/voltage; quantization is accuracy- or layer-sensitivity-driven, not temperature-driven). No matching paper-like title appeared.

recheck_searches.query: temperature-adaptive precision scaling on-device large language model inference sustained throughput throttling
